rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions/guarantees involving ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Use component type annexes for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees when concrete bounds or visible relations are provided.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: type annexes for component-level contracts, implementation annexes for implementation-specific bindings.
Do not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like timing or unit tokens directly into AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
Ensure type consistency between AGREE declarations and AADL data types when performing comparisons.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use visible feature names and valid AADL data types in AGREE expressions.
Boolean expressions should only be used for Boolean ports in scope; avoid converting opaque data to integers.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid in that scope.
Do not assume AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
